summary: The Carnot efficiency is the highest fraction of heat any engine can turn into work between two temperatures: η = 1 − T_c/T_h. It is an ideal ceiling, never quite reached.
method: Identify the hot reservoir temperature T_h and the cold one T_c.
Convert both temperatures to kelvin — the formula only works on the absolute scale.
Decide which quantity is unknown — efficiency, T_c, or T_h.
Rearrange η = 1 − T_c/T_h for the unknown.
Substitute; the efficiency comes out as a fraction between 0 and 1 (multiply by 100 for a percentage).

example.title: Best possible efficiency of a steam engine
steps: The unknown is η, so use η = 1 − T_c/T_h.
